intro: Aneurolepidium pseudoagropyrum, also known as "Tibetan wheat," is a perennial grass native to the Tibetan Plateau and the Himalayas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Aneurolepidium pseudoagropyrum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
